issues.title: Every huh form (single-card confirm AND the merged release-name form) can never be submitted via Enter/Tab/y — the top-level Bubble Tea Update() drops every non-KeyMsg message while in modeForm, so FR5 launches are unreachable live
issues.description:
Live tmux repro on a fixture board: select 2 ready cards + `d` opens the merged huh form (release-name input defaulted correctly to "ready" via suggestRelease, confirm summary correct). Pressing Enter/Tab in the input field never advances focus to the Confirm control (verified with `tmux capture-pane -e`: the reverse-video text-cursor and Left/Right cursor movement stay inside the textinput after Enter/Tab; typing keeps appending characters to the input). Deselecting to a SINGLE ready card and pressing `d` opens the plain Confirm-only form (no input field, so no inter-field navigation is even needed): pressing `y` correctly flips the bound bool (the "Launch" pill visibly highlights via `c.accessor.Set(true)` inside field_confirm.go, which mutates the field synchronously), but pressing Enter ("enter submit") afterwards does NOT complete the form — the screen stays on the confirm view forever, and `tmux list-sessions` confirms no `gogo-done-*`/`gogo-go-*` session was ever created, i.e. `launch.Launch` was never called. The ONLY key that has any effect is Ctrl+C, which huh's `Form.Update` intercepts synchronously (`f.keymap.Quit`) and aborts — so cancelling always works, confirming never does.

Root cause (read in cli/internal/tui/update.go): the top-level `Model.Update(msg tea.Msg)` only recognises `tea.WindowSizeMsg`, `watcherReadyMsg`, `reloadMsg`, `launchDoneMsg`, and `tea.KeyMsg` explicitly; a `tea.KeyMsg` while `m.mode == modeForm` is forwarded to `m.updateForm(msg)` (update.go:44-45), but ANY OTHER message type falls through to the final `if m.mode == modeViewer {...}; return m, nil` (update.go:55-60) — which only handles `modeViewer`, so for `modeForm` it silently drops the message. huh's own field-advancement/submit mechanism is NOT synchronous: `Input.Update`/`Confirm.Update` (huh v1.0.0 field_input.go:350-356, field_confirm.go:210-217) return a deferred `NextField` `tea.Cmd`; the host Bubble Tea program must execute that Cmd and feed the resulting `nextFieldMsg` BACK into `Update()` for `Group.Update`'s `case nextFieldMsg: cmds = append(cmds, g.nextField()...)` (huh group.go:276-278) to actually move focus or, on the last field, complete the group (→ `huh.StateCompleted`). Because gogo's own `Model.Update` never routes `nextFieldMsg` (or `nextGroupMsg`, `updateFieldMsg`, cursor-blink ticks, …) to `m.form.Update(...)` when `mode==modeForm`, this round-trip is broken for every huh form in the app — the plan/in-progress "m" confirm, the ready "d" confirm, and the merged release-name+confirm group. `updateForm`'s own signature (`func (m Model) updateForm(msg tea.KeyMsg)`, update.go:178) is typed to `tea.KeyMsg` specifically, confirming it was never wired to receive the async messages the library needs.

Impact: FR5 ("column moves launch Claude") — the entire reason the board exists — cannot be completed by a user in a real interactive session. A user can select cards, see the exact right confirmation summary and the correctly-suggested release name, but can never actually launch anything; the only reachable outcome is Cancel. This slipped past `go test ./...` (the tui suite only unit-tests `attemptAction`'s pure guard logic — grep confirms no test drives a `tea.KeyMsg` through `modeForm` to `huh.StateCompleted` — see cli/internal/tui/tui_test.go) and past review round 3's manual probe, which replicated `launch.Launch`'s argv directly with a stand-alone script rather than driving the live form via keystrokes (review-02.md: "replicated launch.Launch's argv ... with an argv-dumping script").

Separately verified the launch PRIMITIVE itself is correct once reached: a temporary test that called `launch.BuildIntent`+`launch.Launch` directly (added and removed for this probe; not committed) with a PATH-stubbed `claude` created tmux session `gogo-done-a` and the stub received exactly ONE argv element, `/gogo:done a+b` (injection-safe, no shell splitting) — so the fix is scoped to the message-routing bug above, not to the launch mechanics.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In cli/internal/tui/update.go's top-level `Update`, forward every message to the form when `m.mode == modeForm`, not just `tea.KeyMsg` — mirror the existing `modeViewer` fallback pattern. E.g. change the final fallback to a mode switch: `if m.mode == modeForm { return m.updateForm(msg) }` / `if m.mode == modeViewer { ... }`, and widen `updateForm`'s parameter from `tea.KeyMsg` to `tea.Msg` (it already narrows to `tea.KeyMsg`-specific behaviour only inside the `huh.StateCompleted`/`StateAborted` switch, which is msg-type-agnostic). Also route `m.form.Init()`'s own returned Cmd's messages the same way (currently would hit the same drop). Add a regression test that drives a full form to completion through `Update` using `tea.Sequence`-style manual Cmd execution (call `Init()`, execute the returned `tea.Cmd`, feed the resulting `tea.Msg` back into `Update`, repeat for the Enter keypress) so a future refactor can't reintroduce a message-type gap silently.
issues.fix_summary: Two-part fix. (1) Routing: the top-level Update() no longer drops non-KeyMsg messages while in modeForm — the final fallback now switches on mode and forwards EVERY message to updateForm (WindowSizeMsg also reaches the form for layout), and updateForm's signature was widened tea.KeyMsg -> tea.Msg. This restores the huh round-trip: pressing a key emits async nextFieldMsg/nextGroupMsg batches that must re-enter Update to advance and complete the form. (2) The routing fix exposed a second blocker: the fields were bound via .Value(&m.confirm)/.Value(&m.releaseName) into the value-type Model, which is copied on every Update, so huh wrote confirm=true into an orphaned copy while the completion check read a stale false and silently cancelled. Fixed by moving the field targets behind a heap-stable *formBinding pointer shared across all model copies. The confirm now defaults to the affirmative so Enter/Tab submits-and-launches; Esc (newly intercepted in updateForm) and Ctrl+C abort. Regression tests drive the full lifecycle through Update — pumping the returned command graph (expanding tea.Batch, re-feeding each msg) to huh.StateCompleted — and assert an injected fake launcher fires exactly once, for BOTH the single-confirm ship and the merged release-name form. Live tmux proof (scratch fixture + PATH-injected claude stub): 2 ready cards -> d -> Enter (advance) -> Enter (Launch) launched, stub received a SINGLE argv element '/gogo:done board-actions-and-filter+pipeline-commands'; gate: gofmt clean, go vet clean, go test -race green. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; live tmux proof — Enter-submit launched the stub with single-argv '/gogo:done a+b', Esc-cancel cleared selection without launching]

issues.title: Cancelling/aborting a launch form doesn't clear the ready-card multi-selection, so a later, unrelated `m`/`d` press silently re-ships the stale selection instead of acting on the newly-focused card
issues.description: Live repro: select 2 ready cards (ready-alpha + ready-beta) + `d` → merged form opens → Ctrl+C aborts → board returns with `status="cancelled"`, but both cards still show `[x]` (m.selected untouched). Navigating away to a completely different, unselected card (plan-only, class unfinished) and pressing `m` (intending to move THAT card to implement) instead reopens "will run: claude \"/gogo:done ready-beta\" ..." — the stale ship of the old selection — because `attemptAction` (cli/internal/tui/move.go:23-27) checks `m.selectedSlugs()` FIRST, unconditionally, before ever looking at the focused card or the `ship` bool: `sel := m.selectedSlugs(); if len(sel) > 0 { return launch.BuildIntent(launch.ActionDone, sel, ""), true, "" }`. `updateForm`'s `StateAborted` branch (and the `StateCompleted && !m.confirm` "cancelled" branch) only reset `m.mode` and `m.status` (update.go:188-198) — neither clears `m.selected`. Per the code's own doc comment (`ship=false (m): plan/unfinished -> go ...`), `m` is documented to have per-class behaviour with no mention of selection; the blanket selection-first check silently overrides that documented behaviour whenever a selection lingers, which after any cancel it always does. This is a real correctness/safety risk once TEST-001 is fixed and forms can actually complete: a user could cancel a ship, believe it's abandoned (selection checkmarks are easy to miss under the currently-focused column), then confirm what they think is an unrelated `m`/`d` action and actually launch `/gogo:done` against the old, unintended slugs.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Clear `m.selected = map[string]bool{}` in `updateForm`'s `huh.StateAborted` case and in the `StateCompleted && !m.confirm` ("cancelled") case, mirroring the clear that already happens on a successful `doLaunch` (move.go:112-113). Optionally also make `attemptAction`'s selection short-circuit apply only when `ship == true` (since `m`'s documented contract is per-class, not selection-driven), so a stray selection can never hijack a `m` move even before the form opens.
issues.fix_summary: A new cancelForm() helper (called on Esc/Ctrl+C abort AND on completion via the Cancel/n control) clears the whole launch-in-progress state — pending intent, pendingShip, the *formBinding, the *huh.Form, AND the ready-card selection — and sets a one-line 'cancelled' status, so a stale unconfirmed target can never be silently re-shipped. The successful-launch path now also clears selection+pending on the RETURNED model (the prior clear lived only on doLaunch's discarded value-receiver copy, so it never took effect). A fresh m/d therefore always rebuilds its target list from the CURRENT selection/focus. Regression test TestFormAbortClearsSelectionNoRelaunch: abort a merged ship via Ctrl+C, assert the launcher was NOT called, m.selectedSlugs() is empty, and a subsequent m on a plan card yields a go intent (not the abandoned done) — proving no resurrection. Live tmux proof: after Esc the board showed 'cancelled', the [x] markers were gone, and the stub call-count did not increase. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green; live tmux proof — Enter-submit launched the stub with single-argv '/gogo:done a+b', Esc-cancel cleared selection without launching]

issues.title: 'v' freezes the whole UI for minutes before opening the view
issues.description: User report (live UAT on the board): pressing v on a card blocks the entire TUI for minutes before the viewer appears. Likely a synchronous heavy render inside Update() (glamour render of a large markdown, ASCII diagram layout on a big .mmd, or pathological lipgloss wrapping) running on the UI goroutine. Needs root-cause + async render (tea.Cmd + spinner/loading state) and/or caching; opening a view must feel instant.
issues.fix_summary: Root cause (MEASURED, not guessed): renderMarkdown built the glamour renderer with WithAutoStyle() SYNCHRONOUSLY inside Update() on the UI goroutine. WithAutoStyle -> glamour.getDefaultStyle -> termenv.HasDarkBackground() -> termStatusReport writes an OSC-11 background + CSI-6n cursor query to the TTY and BLOCKS reading the reply via waitForData(OSCTimeout=5s). Bubble Tea already owns stdin, so the reply is consumed by BT's input reader and termenv times out the full 5s per query — re-run on EVERY view (a fresh renderer each time). The UI goroutine wedges and nothing repaints: the 'minutes' freeze. (go test saw ~4ms because it has no TTY, so getDefaultStyle short-circuits to NoTTYStyleConfig — which is why unit tests never caught it.) Fix, three parts: (1) detect the terminal background ONCE in New() (before tea.Program grabs the TTY) via lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(), stored on the model; markdown now renders with an EXPLICIT WithStandardStyle(dark|light) so there is ZERO terminal query in the hot path. (2) ALL viewer rendering (markdown/issues/events/diagram) moved off the UI goroutine into a tea.Cmd, with an immediate modeViewer 'rendering…' state + a bubbles/spinner; content returns via viewerContentMsg. (3) rendered output cached per (kind|path|width) so reopening is instant, and a WindowSizeMsg re-renders only when the width actually changed. Model test TestViewerAsyncLoadingThenContent asserts the immediate loading state, async content delivery, and cache-hit-on-reopen (nil cmd). LIVE tmux proof: fixed binary opens the ready report at 15ms (loading state) / 43-85ms (content); the round-5 binary under IDENTICAL TERM=xterm-256color stayed frozen on the board with the viewer never appearing within 12s. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green (64 tests), WithAutoStyle gone from hot path, live tmux evidence in fix report (v: 15ms loading/43ms content; per-column defaults; ASCII fences; session dot; card highlight)]

issues.title: 'v' opens plan.md regardless of column — default file must follow the column
issues.description: v on a ready-column card opens the PLAN view. Wanted default per column: ready -> report/report.md (or legacy report.md); plan -> plan.md; in progress -> the file list (drill-in); changelog -> the entry's report.md. If the column's default file does not exist, open the file list instead.
issues.fix_summary: quickView (v) now opens the DEFAULT file for the focused card's COLUMN instead of always plan.md. New defaultArtifact() maps: ready -> f.ReportPath (report/report.md, else legacy root report.md); plan/unfinished -> plan.md; changelog -> the changelog entry's report.md (with the feature's own report bundle as a secondary fallback for shipped-by-status features); in progress -> the drill-in file list (no file). A missing default also opens the file list. enter (openDrill) is unchanged everywhere — always the file list. Table test TestQuickViewDefaultsPerColumn covers all four columns plus the missing-default -> file-list fallback. LIVE tmux: v on ready opened report/report.md, v on plan opened plan.md, v on in-progress showed 'files — inprogress' (the list), v on a changelog card opened the entry report.md, and a shipped-by-status card with no report fell back to the file list. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green (64 tests), WithAutoStyle gone from hot path, live tmux evidence in fix report (v: 15ms loading/43ms content; per-column defaults; ASCII fences; session dot; card highlight)]

issues.title: Mermaid fences inside plan/report markdown render as raw DSL — replace with ASCII diagrams
issues.description: Plans/reports embed ```mermaid fences; the glamour view shows the raw DSL source. In the terminal markdown view, pre-process fences: flowchart-family -> the internal ASCII renderer output (as an indented block); non-flowchart kinds -> keep the source block but label it (kind + 'press w for the browser view').
issues.fix_summary: New preprocessMermaid() rewrites ```mermaid fences BEFORE glamour renders (glamour was showing the raw DSL). Flowchart-family sources -> the internal diagram.Render ASCII inside a plain ``` code block, prefixed with a derivable title (--- title: / %% title:). Other kinds (sequence/class/state/er/…, classified by new diagram.Kind) -> a labeled block '[mermaid <kind> — press w for the browser view]' followed by the source in a code block. Malformed/unrenderable DSL NEVER crashes — safeFlowchart recovers any panic and degrades to the labeled source. Unit tests: a flow fence -> ASCII (boxes/arrows) present and the raw ```mermaid marker gone; sequenceDiagram -> label + preserved source; an unrenderable flowchart AND pure garbage -> label + source with no panic; non-mermaid markdown passes through byte-for-byte. LIVE tmux: the ready report's flowchart fence rendered as boxes-and-arrows and its sequenceDiagram fence as the '[mermaid sequence — press w …]' labeled source — no raw ```mermaid anywhere in the viewport. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green (64 tests), WithAutoStyle gone from hot path, live tmux evidence in fix report (v: 15ms loading/43ms content; per-column defaults; ASCII fences; session dot; card highlight)]

issues.title: Board cards do not show which item has an active tmux session
issues.description: No visual cue that a card has an attachable gogo-* session. Add an indicator on the card (green dot + 'session' hint or similar) refreshed with the board reload, so the user knows 'a' will attach.
issues.fix_summary: Cards now show an unmissable green ● session marker whenever a live gogo-* tmux session matches the slug, and the board status line surfaces '● <slug> has a live session — press a to attach' for the focused card. The session set refreshes on each board reload AND on a new modest 5s tea.Tick ticker (sessionTick -> sessionsMsg, with tmux listed off the UI goroutine) so the dots never go stale between fsnotify writes. Test TestSessionIndicatorOnCard asserts the dot + attach hint appear with a live session and disappear without one. LIVE tmux: created a real 'gogo-go-ready' session running sleep 300 in the scratch fixture -> within one ticker interval the ready card changed from '○ ready' to '○ ready  ● session' and the status line showed the attach hint (no board reload needed); killing the session cleared the dot on the next tick. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green (64 tests), WithAutoStyle gone from hot path, live tmux evidence in fix report (v: 15ms loading/43ms content; per-column defaults; ASCII fences; session dot; card highlight)]

issues.title: Items look like plain list rows — make them cards, highlight the whole selected card, add color
issues.description: Columns render as text lists; wanted: bordered card look per item (lipgloss boxes), the WHOLE focused card highlighted (not just a line), clearer visual identity per item, and a richer (still tasteful, TONE-like) color scheme across the UI (column accents, badges, selection).
issues.fix_summary: The board is restyled into bordered cards (new styles.go — every style precomputed ONCE at init, no per-frame rebuild). Each item is a lipgloss rounded-border card at a fixed column width showing the slug (bold), a truncated title line, and the badge line (phase/round · waiting-for-user · ● session). The FOCUSED card gets a full-card highlight — accent border + subtle adaptive background, rendered with plain inner text so the fill has no per-segment background holes — that moves with the cursor; selected-for-ship cards get the select accent border + a ✓. Per-column accents adapt the xplan TONE palette via lipgloss AdaptiveColor (plan #7aa8ff / in-progress #e6a14a / ready #5db97a / changelog muted) so light terminals stay readable, and column headers are colored to match with counts. Existing substring View tests stay green (go test has no TTY, so lipgloss emits plain text). LIVE tmux capture: four bordered card columns with colored headers + counts; the whole-card highlight followed the cursor down (unfinished -> aborted on Down), and ready cards showed the ○/✓ ship markers. [orchestrator-verified: -race suite green (64 tests), WithAutoStyle gone from hot path, live tmux evidence in fix report (v: 15ms loading/43ms content; per-column defaults; ASCII fences; session dot; card highlight)]

issues.title: ASCII diagram rendering quality is poor — adopt mermaid-ascii's renderer (Unicode box-drawing)
issues.description: User UAT round 2: the internal minimal ASCII renderer's output 'doesnt looks good'. Reference: HN 46804828 — AlexanderGrooff/mermaid-ascii (MIT, v1.3.0, actively maintained) renders mermaid as clean Unicode box-drawing (--ascii fallback), supports flowcharts AND sequence diagrams, and keeps its layout/drawing logic in pkg/ separate from the cobra/gin cmd surface (so importing pkg/ does not link gin into the binary). Replace the internal renderer with the library (or vendor its pkg files with MIT attribution if transitive imports prove unavoidable); Unicode default; extend terminal rendering to sequence diagrams; class/state keep the labeled-source fallback.
issues.fix_summary: Replaced the internal minimal ASCII flowchart renderer with the mermaid-ascii engine (github.com/AlexanderGrooff/mermaid-ascii, MIT, tag 1.3.0 / commit aca241698ec7) — Unicode box-drawing by default plus real sequenceDiagram support. IMPORT-vs-VENDOR was decided EMPIRICALLY, not assumed: a probe binary + `go mod why gin` + `go version -m` proved that importing upstream's clean pkg/sequence + pkg/diagram links ZERO gin/sonic/cobra into the binary — so the SEQUENCE renderer is imported directly. Upstream's GRAPH renderer, however, lives in package `cmd` next to cmd/web.go (which imports gin-gonic), and Go links whole packages — so the graph render path is VENDORED into cli/internal/diagram/mermaidascii/ with the upstream MIT LICENSE + attribution in doc.go, trimmed to the render files only: the cobra/gin cmd surface is excluded, logrus is replaced by a no-op logger (log.go), and gookit/color is dropped (wrapTextInColor emits plain text — no ANSI inside code blocks). New facade cli/internal/diagram/ascii.go exposes Render(source, width) (string, error): Unicode for flowchart/graph AND sequenceDiagram; class/state/er/etc. return ErrUnsupported so callers keep the labeled 'press w for the browser view' fallback. sanitizeFlowchart strips classDef/class/style/linkStyle (they otherwise render as stray nodes); sanitizeSequence maps actor->participant and drops Note/loop/alt/opt/etc. so real-world sequences render their participants+arrows instead of erroring; Render never panics (recover at the package boundary). BOTH consumption points now use the engine: the drill-in .mmd viewer (renderDiagramFile, now width-plumbed) and the markdown fence pre-processor (preprocessMermaid/renderMermaidBlock) — sequence fences upgraded from labeled-source to REAL rendering. The old internal box/topoOrder renderer was deleted. Tests: the diagram package asserts Unicode boxes+arrows for a flow fixture, a sequence fixture renders (incl. actor/Note sanitize), class/er -> ErrUnsupported, and empty/comment-only/garbage -> fallback with no panic; the markdown tests assert flow+sequence fences -> Unicode (raw ```mermaid gone), class -> labeled source, malformed/garbage -> labeled source; a vendored-package smoke test covers Unicode/ASCII/no-panic. BINARY CHECK: `go version -m /tmp/gogo-f7` contains NO gin/sonic/cobra/logrus/gookit; size delta only +270KB vs a stubbed-diagram baseline (mermaid-ascii pkg/sequence + orderedmap/v2 + the vendored graph); startup unaffected (~10-20ms `--version`). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet clean, go test -race ./... green. LIVE tmux proof (session gogo-test-f7, read-only on the real repo): drill-in charts/cli-cockpit-flow.mmd rendered as Unicode boxes with routed arrows (classDef stripped, no leaked nodes); report/sequence.mmd rendered all participants with solid/dotted/self-message arrows (actor U sanitized to a participant, Note over dropped, no error); and report/report.md's embedded flowchart + sequence fences rendered as Unicode inside the glamour markdown view with no raw ```mermaid anywhere. state.md untouched. [orchestrator-verified: -race green; live Unicode captures for flow+sequence; go version -m clean of gin/sonic/cobra; +270KB, startup unaffected. Orchestrator also closed the third markdown surface: gogo view stdout now runs the same fence preprocessor (0 raw fences).]

issues.title: Terminal markdown view: cramped — bigger spacing + highlight important content
issues.description: UAT round 3: plan/report views feel dense. Tune the glamour style config (explicit dark/light styles we already pass): larger heading margins/blank lines between blocks, clearer heading hierarchy (color/bold per level), stronger emphasis for bold/inline-code, comfortable document margin — a readable article feel in the terminal.
issues.fix_summary: Replaced the stock WithStandardStyle(dark|light) with a CUSTOM glamour StyleConfig built once per variant (new cli/internal/tui/mdstyle.go): derived from glamour's Dark/Light config (so code-block chroma etc. stay sensible), then tuned for an article feel — a ~2 document margin; generous block spacing (a trailing blank line after paragraphs and lists via BlockSuffix, and a blank line ABOVE every heading via Heading.BlockPrefix, which cascades to H1–H6); a clear heading hierarchy on the board's TONE accents (H1 bold blue #7aa8ff/#2f6fe0 as a clean accent title — glamour's inverse H1 block dropped; H2 green; H3 amber; H4 faint); stronger emphasis (Strong brighter/bolder than body) and inline code on a subtle background fill (#242a36/#eef1f8) in an accent color; and a calm italic blockquote with a colored bar plus a quieter rule. The config is built with FRESH pointers only, so glamour's shared package vars are never mutated. Applied in BOTH surfaces via the shared tui.MarkdownStyle(dark): the TUI async renderer (renderMarkdownContent) and the non-interactive `gogo view` stdout path (view.go, dark detected once via lipgloss.HasDarkBackground). Still zero terminal queries in the hot path (no WithAutoStyle — TEST-003 stays fixed). Tests: TestMarkdownStyleConfig asserts the margin, block-spacing, distinct heading colors, code background, and bold in both variants + that dark≠light and glamour's package var is untouched; TestMarkdownStyleRendersAnsiAndSpacing asserts real ANSI + blank-line block spacing (ANSI-stripped) in the render. Gates: gofmt clean, go vet clean, go test -race ./... green. [orchestrator-verified: -race green (+6 tests), embedded assets byte-identical, TL;DR guidance in all 3 authoring surfaces, headless-Chrome modal smoke PASS]

issues.title: Viewer scrolling is slow — add page-by-page scrolling
issues.description: UAT round 3: only line scrolling exists and it is slow. Wire viewport paging: PgDn/PgUp + space/b = full page, d/u = half page, g/G = top/bottom; keep j/k lines. Ensure scroll is smooth on large rendered docs (no re-render per keypress — scroll the cached render).
issues.fix_summary: updateViewer now wires the full paging set on the bubbles/viewport: PgDn/PgUp + space/b = full page (PageDown/PageUp), d/u (+ctrl+d/ctrl+u) = half page (HalfPageDown/HalfPageUp), g/G = top/bottom (GotoTop/GotoBottom); j/k + arrows keep line scroll (forwarded to the viewport keymap). PERF: every one of these only moves the viewport's yOffset over its CACHED, already-rendered lines — bubbles/viewport slices m.lines, so there is NO per-keypress re-render (the width-keyed render cache is only touched on an actual width change). KEY-CONFLICT resolution as required: `space` and `d`/`u` act as paging ONLY in the viewer context — on the board `space`=select and `d`=ship are untouched (they route through updateBoard before ever reaching updateViewer); the `G`=glow shortcut was MOVED from the viewer to the FILE-LIST (drill) context (updateDrill now maps G→openInGlow on the highlighted file), freeing `G` in the open viewer for GotoBottom while glow stays reachable from the list. Footer/help updated: the viewer line now reads '↑↓/jk line · space/b page · d/u ½page · g/G top/bottom · w web · esc back · (glow: G from the file list)', and the file-list line gained 'G glow'. Tests: TestViewerPagingKeys (half/full page, g/G, pgdn, u, j line), TestViewerPagingDoesNotRerender (no command spawned + render cache untouched on a scroll key), TestBoardShipAndSelectUnaffected (board d still ships, space still selects), TestGlowContextIsFileListNotViewer (G in the list routes to glow, G in the viewer goes to bottom). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet clean, go test -race ./... green. [orchestrator-verified: -race green (+6 tests), embedded assets byte-identical, TL;DR guidance in all 3 authoring surfaces, headless-Chrome modal smoke PASS]

issues.title: Plans and reports should always END with a summary section
issues.description: UAT round 3 (plugin-side): add authoring guidance + template so every plan.md and report.md closes with a final '## Summary (TL;DR)' section — a few bold lines: what/why/state/next. gogo-plan + gogo-knowledge skills + templates/report.template.md. Existing artifacts unchanged.
issues.fix_summary: Plugin-side authoring guidance + template. gogo-plan/SKILL.md: the plan.md shape (Step 3) gains a FINAL '## Summary (TL;DR)' section — 3-5 bold-led lines (what's being built · why · the chosen approach · what happens next) — and the article-style guidance block gained a matching '- Close with ## Summary (TL;DR)' bullet (the D4=A 'no new sections' note now reads 'not new sections beyond the closing ## Summary (TL;DR)'). gogo-knowledge/SKILL.md (Step 3): the report.md section list gains a final '## Summary (TL;DR)' item (what shipped · review + test verdicts one line each · follow-ups pointer) with the same article-guidance bullet + D4=A wording tweak. templates/report.template.md: a new closing '## Summary (TL;DR)' section appended after 'Follow-ups & known limitations', with a fill-in prompt for the closing recap; the template's existing structure is otherwise untouched. docs/cli-contract.md was CHECKED and left untouched — it documents the file surface (that plan.md/report.md exist) but does NOT enumerate their internal section headings, so per the instruction it does not promise a section list. Existing plans/reports unchanged (authoring-time guidance only). [orchestrator-verified: -race green (+6 tests), embedded assets byte-identical, TL;DR guidance in all 3 authoring surfaces, headless-Chrome modal smoke PASS]

issues.title: Web viewer: side-by-side compare diagrams too small — add expand-to-modal
issues.description: UAT round 3 (web): compare rows render two diagrams per row and each is hard to read. Add an 'expand' control per figure that opens the diagram in a near-fullscreen modal overlay (same interactivity — rich renderer or pan/zoom fallback — refit on open), Esc/click-outside/close-button to dismiss, figure returns to its row and refits. assets/viewer JS/CSS; offline; ALSO resync the CLI's embedded copies (cli/internal/pages/assets) and rebuild.
issues.fix_summary:
Added an expand-to-modal to assets/viewer (plain window.gogoViewer scripts, no modules). MECHANICS: a single shared, lazily-created overlay (a fixed dark-scrim .gogo-modal-scrim at z-index 9999 holding a ~95vw/92vh .gogo-modal panel with a ✕ .gogo-modal-close and a .gogo-modal-body). Each figure.diagram gets a ⛶ '.gogo-expand' button appended to its .controls strip (added in interactive.js after render, so it works for BOTH renderer kinds). On click, modal.open MOVES that figure's .viewport DOM node into the modal body (not a re-render — state survives), shows the scrim, and REFITS via a double-rAF: the refit entry point is each renderer's own fit — render.js (rich flowchart) now returns { viewport, redraw, resetLayout, refit:()=>{fitAll();draw()} } and fallbackViewport (pan/zoom) now returns { viewport, refit:fit }, so both re-fit to the new modal size using their existing getBoundingClientRect-based fit. Close via ✕, Esc (document keydown), or a scrim/backdrop click (target===scrim) moves the node back to its figure (appendChild restores the after-figcaption position) and refits to the original size. Only ONE modal at a time — opening a second closes the first (returns its node to its row). CSS: .gogo-modal-body .viewport overrides the in-row 70vh sizing to fill the panel. Pure offline DOM, no network, file://-safe. RESYNC: `make sync-assets` copied the changed interactive.js/render.js/viewer.css into cli/internal/pages/assets/ (byte-identical — verified: only mermaid.min.js differs, and it is the exempt embed-only file) and `go build` produced /tmp/gogo-f8; a fresh `gogo view :report --web` writes .gogo/resources/viewer/* from the embedded FS and those served copies carry the modal machinery byte-for-byte. EVIDENCE: node --check clean on both JS files; a headless Google-Chrome DOM smoke (stubbed mermaid → deterministic fallback path, two figures) asserted expand-control presence, open→node-in-modal, Esc-close→node-restored, scrim-click close, ✕ close, and the one-modal-at-a-time invariant — 
PASS
. [orchestrator-verified: -race green (+6 tests), embedded assets byte-identical, TL;DR guidance in all 3 authoring surfaces, headless-Chrome modal smoke PASS]

issues.title: Launched claude session parks on first-run MCP trust prompt (cwd not anchored to repo root)
issues.description: UAT: gogo:done launched from the board (run from cli/) spawned claude with cwd=cli/ — Claude Code treats that dir as a new project, so the session parked on the '2 new MCP servers found' prompt despite existing approvals at the repo root. Fix: tmux new-session gains `-c <root>` and the claude -p fallback sets cmd.Dir=root, anchoring every launch to the .gogo repo root where .claude/settings.local.json approvals live.
issues.fix_summary: TmuxNewSessionArgs(root, in) adds -c root; background path sets cmd.Dir=root; test updated with the anchoring rationale. [orchestrator-fixed inline + verified: -race suite green, binary rebuilt 0.10.0. Workaround for an already-parked session: tmux attach → Enter to accept → Ctrl+b d.]

issues.title: Board columns don't scroll — long card lists get cut off at terminal height
issues.description: UAT round 4: after shipping, the changelog column holds 9 cards; columns render all cards top-down and anything beyond the viewport height is cut off (screenshot evidence). Add per-column vertical windowing/scrolling: keep the focused card visible (auto-scroll window follows the cursor), render only the cards that fit, with overflow indicators ('^ N more' above / 'v N more' below) and a scroll position hint; works per column independently; handles tiny terminals; filter + selection unaffected.
issues.fix_summary: Per-column vertical windowing (new cli/internal/tui/window.go; Model gains colOffset[4]). On render each column MEASURES every card's height with lipgloss.Height (cards are multi-line bordered lipgloss boxes — heights are measured, not assumed, so a taller card still windows right) and shows only the WHOLE cards that fit in the column's row budget colAvail = height-5 (terminal height minus the board chrome header/status/help = 3 and the column's own head+blank = 2). fitEnd() packs whole cards from the offset, reserving one row for a '↑ N more' indicator when start>0 and one for '↓ N more' when cards remain hidden below (the bottom-indicator row is resolved by an optimistic-then-reserve two-pass so counts never overflow the budget), and ALWAYS shows ≥1 card — so a terminal too short for even one card degrades to the focused card + indicators, never a negative slice index, never a panic. CLAMP RULES: reflowColumns() runs on every board-affecting transition (nav, filter, reload, resize). The FOCUSED column scrolls-into-view via scrollWindow(): snap the offset up to the cursor when it is above the window, advance one card at a time when it is below, so the focused card is always fully visible. NON-focused columns keep their offset (independent scrolling), only re-clamped to lastFitStart() — the max offset that still seats the last card at the bottom, so no empty trailing space; an over-scrolled offset after a filter shrink is pulled back, an empty column pins offset 0. fsnotify reloads capture the focused slug, reload, then refocus() restores the cursor to that slug if it survives (else clamps) before the reflow, so it stays visible. A per-column POSITION HINT (e.g. 'changelog (9) · 4–5') is appended to the column header, shown ONLY when the column overflows (no noise on short columns), alongside the '↑/↓ N more' lines. TESTS (new window_test.go): pure fitEnd across varying heights + a tall card among short ones; scrollWindow cursor-follow (down shifts the window one card, up snaps, the last card lands fully visible, non-focused keeps/clamps its offset); lastFitStart max-offset; tiny-height degradation (≥1 card, valid slice, no panic); empty column; and model/View integration — indicators+hint HIDDEN when all cards fit, SHOWN with correct N-counts and range when windowed, scroll-into-view keeps the focused card visible walking to the bottom while hiding the rest, a filter shrink clamps the offset to 0, refocus follows/clamps a slug, a reloadMsg reflows without panic and keeps the focused card visible. GATES: gofmt -l clean, go vet clean, go test -race ./... green. LIVE tmux (session gogo-test-f9, `tmux new-session -x 200 -y 20` on the REAL repo with 9 shipped changelog cards): the whole board fits in 20 rows; changelog shows '· 1–2' + two cards + '↓ 7 more'; scrolling down the focused column the window FOLLOWS the cursor (mid: '· 4–5' with both '↑ 3 more' and '↓ 4 more') and the last card ('pipeline-commands') becomes fully visible at '· 8–9' with '↑ 7 more' and no '↓'. state.md untouched. [orchestrator-verified: -race green (11 new windowing tests); live capture at 20 rows shows header range hint (changelog (9) · 4–5), both ↑/↓ N more indicators, cursor-follow to the last fully-visible card]
